The Particles

Particles (Μόρια) are small, indeclinable words that don't fit easily into other categories but play a vital role in Greek syntax, specifically in forming future tenses, subjunctive moods, and expressing emphasis.

  • θα - Future marker (will)
  • να - Subjunctive marker (to / let)
  • ας - Exhortative marker (let / let it be)
  • μα - Emphatic "but" or "though"

Usage Examples

Θα πάω. (I will go.)

Θέλω να πάω. (I want to go.)

Ας πάμε! (Let's go!)

Congratulations!

You have reached the end of the Formal Grammar section of the Modern Greek curriculum. You now have a solid foundation in phonology, morphology, and basic syntax. Next, dive into the Practical Phrases and Dialogues to see all this grammar in action!
